task.title: Move bootstrap and prompt templates into CodeWiki skill assets
task.summary: Make the CodeWiki-product vs CodeWiki-dogfood boundary explicit while moving bootstrap, /wiki-resume prompt templates, and starter template assets to the CodeWiki skill asset boundary.

goal.outcome: Agents cannot confuse .codewiki/ dogfood state with package source, and bootstrap/resume prompt/template assets are owned by the CodeWiki skill while source executes them through application APIs.
acceptance: Repo prompt/skill/template language explicitly distinguishes dogfood state, generated state, runtime state, and package source.
Starter templates include canonical/generated/runtime path taxonomy guidance.
Bootstrap workflow guidance and prompt templates live under the CodeWiki skill asset boundary, with source code only exposing executable application tools or compatibility loaders.
/wiki-resume injected implementation prompts are rendered from skill-owned templates/assets rather than hard-coded application prompt text.
/wiki-resume preserves or incorporates user-supplied follow-up intent after command invocation instead of discarding it.

Resume prompt templates include selected task, artifact statuses, conflicting artifacts and holders/waiters when relevant, temporary session usage record, and helper-safe next steps for parallel sessions.
Packaged skills include required bootstrap/prompt/template assets and smoke tests prove package reachability.
Architecture/audit check detects active docs/source that call .codewiki/ package source or generated task views canonical truth.
non_goals: Do not make skills import adapters or own product semantics.
Do not remove compatibility loaders before packaging and migration tests pass.
Do not implement session queue/artifact-status scheduling policy in the template layer; TASK-086 owns allocation semantics.
verification: source-owned package/audit check verifies skill assets are reachable
/wiki-resume prompt fixture proves skill-owned template rendering, artifact-status wording, and follow-up intent preservation

delta.desired: Skill assets own bootstrap guidance, prompts, resume prompt templates, and starter templates; source exposes tools/loaders without duplicating workflow policy.
delta.current: Bootstrap/templates/prompts are split across package source and skill docs; /wiki-resume prompt text is source-owned and can discard user follow-up text.
delta.closure: Skill-owned assets are packaged, /wiki-resume renders skill-owned templates using artifact-status wording while preserving follow-up intent, source callers use shared application tools/loaders, and dogfood/source boundary checks pass.
